target.writeBoundary: No file or directory under C:/Users/ytkim/Desktop/kyt_work may be modified by this artifact.

directionLock.retrofitRule: observed, inferred, open-question, and user-confirmed states must remain separate
directionLock.authorityBoundary: Only user-confirmed and planned slices may drive implementation.

knownFacts.claim: CardPrinterConfig is a legacy MFC dialog utility for printer configuration workflows.
SMART-51 is represented as a printer group in the CardPrinterConfig code structure.
SMART-51 routing distinguishes Printer, Laminator, Flipper, and MHopper2 module screens.
The utility distinguishes User, Dealer, and Factory grant levels.
Main dialog wrappers centralize hardware read/write operations for printer and modules.
The utility has config save/load behavior and INI-style application settings.

inferredEdges.intentClaim: The selected printer group and connected module status decide which configuration dialog is visible.
Parameter visibility and writeability are expected to depend on User, Dealer, or Factory grant level.
Module-specific parameter screens should route hardware reads and writes through main dialog wrappers instead of owning device IO directly.
Save/load flows are likely expected to preserve supported configuration parameter state without blindly writing inaccessible or hardware-only values.

openQuestions.question: Should CardPrinterConfig be treated primarily as a configuration utility rather than a diagnostic or test utility?
Should Release_User and Release_Factory be modeled as separate personas with different allowed actions?
Is QuickSet still a supported behavior or only a legacy compatibility path?
Should hardware validation be mandatory for any write-path change?

changeRequestIntakeTemplate.exampleRequest: Add a SMART-51 printer setting change.
unknownBeforeUserAnswer: Owning module: Printer, Laminator, Flipper, or MHopper2.
Permission level: User, Dealer, or Factory.
Operation mode: UI-only, device read, device write, or both.
Whether config save/load must include the setting.
changeRequestIntakeTemplate.firstQuestion: What is the exact setting name, UI label, parameter name, or device menu wording for the SMART-51 setting you want to add?

candidateSlices.purpose: Observe and model how device group and module selection routes to CardPrinterConfig subdialogs.
Prepare a future SMART-51 setting change only after the exact setting, module, permission, operation mode, save/load behavior, and hardware validation need are confirmed.

nonGoals: Do not treat inferred retrofit facts as source authority.
Do not generate implementation instructions from open questions.
Do not collapse project-specific intent claims into enum-only values.
